families.notes: Abstraction layer first (split AuthService from session middleware), then upgrade. connect-redis v7→v8 changes cookie format ⇒ forced sign-out at deploy boundary. PROD banner mandatory. dual-runtime allows old+new session cookies to coexist during the canary window.
supabase-js v2 stable. Watch for Realtime v2 transition.
`workspace:*` protocol packages — managed by changesets, not PR-9.
OTel SDK + pino logger track. Deferred until Node 22 alignment. observability is meta — touches log volume, not user-visible SLOs.

BullMQ 5 changes Worker concurrency semantics + repeatable job ID format. Existing repeatable jobs need re-emit. Workers MUST deploy before API (deployment_sequence: workers-first). Rollback runbook MUST cover repeatable-job re-emission in v4 format.

Nest 11 requires Node 20+, Express 5, RxJS 7.x. Must follow PR-9b/c/d/e — upgrade order matters for peer-dep resolution. 72h DEV preprod soak (every module loads through Nest — widest blast radius of PR-9 series). Real risk is Express 5 cascade (Passport / sessions / middleware) — see PR-9f.0.

Spike: parallel platform-fastify branch. NO removal of platform-express. Compare cold-start + p99 latency on /api/_perf endpoint. Blast radius analysis showed Fastify migration would cascade across Passport, sessions, connect-redis, Express middleware, legacy Nest adapters, SEO routes — larger than Nest 11 itself. Spike informs a future PR-9h decision, NOT a production swap. Empty runtime_budget_constraints is intentional: spike MEASURES perf without GATING (invariant #50 exempts production_approved=false).
Remix v2 → Vite 6 → React 19 chain. Deferred until React 19 stable + Vite 6 ecosystem caught up. Not in PR-9 scope.
Low-risk catch-up batch — defer to a v1.5 sweep.

Bundle typescript + @typescript-eslint/* + eslint together — typed-lint breakage couples them. ESLint 9 flat config tracked in same PR if scope stays manageable, else extracted to PR-9b.2. target_major is intentionally "locked-at-execution" because TS6/TS7-Go ETAs drift faster than this doc.

TypeScript native (Go) port. Track ONLY for build-speed benchmarks on the monorepo. NOT a production candidate. Do NOT conflate with `tooling-typescript-eslint` PR-9b track — this is observation-only. A future agent reading this overlay MUST treat production_approved=false as a hard veto, regardless of CI signal.

Zod 4 ships breaking parser changes (z.string() defaults, error API). Must touch every consumer schema in shared/ + backend/ + frontend/. 24h DEV preprod soak required — forms/DTOs are user-facing surface.
